name: Thomas Wright
complaint: Sudden severe pain in right hip after a fall
summary: Elderly patient presenting with right hip fracture after a mechanical fall at home.

keyFacts.label: Hidden clue
keyFacts.value: Right leg is shortened and externally rotated

expectedQuestions: Can you put any weight on your leg?
hints: Can you describe the position of your leg right now?
Are you able to stand or put weight on it?

scriptedAnswers.answer: I tripped over a rug in the hallway and landed hard on my right side.
No, I can't put any weight on it at all, the pain is unbearable.
My right leg looks a bit shorter than the left, and my foot is pointing outwards.

fallbackResponses: It hurts too much to move.
I just need some pain relief.
Please be careful with my leg.

name: Leo Martinez
summary: A child presenting with an acute asthma exacerbation triggered by a viral infection.
vitals: Temp 37.8 C, HR 130, RR 32, BP 100/65, SpO2 92% on room air
history: Diagnosed with mild intermittent asthma, eczema
expectedQuestions: How long has he been wheezing?
Is he sucking in his chest when breathing?
hints: Ask the parent if the child has used their rescue inhaler.
Ask about chest retractions.

scriptedAnswers.answer: (Parent) I gave him two puffs of salbutamol an hour ago but it didn't help much.
(Parent) Yes, his tummy and ribs seem to suck in deep every time he takes a breath.
(Parent) He started with a runny nose and dry cough yesterday.

fallbackResponses: (Parent) He's really struggling to breathe.
(Parent) Should he be breathing this fast?
(Child) My chest feels tight.
evaluationFocus: Assess severity of pediatric respiratory distress

name: Elena Rostova
complaint: A mole on the back that has changed color
summary: Patient presents with a changing pigmented lesion suspicious for melanoma.
history: Frequent sunburns in childhood, fair skin

keyFacts.label: Onset
keyFacts.value: Noticed changes over the last 3 months

keyFacts.label: Hidden clue
keyFacts.value: Lesion has irregular borders and multiple colors

expectedQuestions: How long has it been changing?
Do you have a family history of skin cancer?
communicationGoals: Reassure while emphasizing need for biopsy
hints: Ask about the ABCDE criteria of the mole.
Ask about childhood sun exposure.

scriptedAnswers.answer: My partner noticed it looked darker and bigger about 3 months ago.
It used to be light brown, now it has black and dark brown spots, and the edges look jagged.
It doesn't hurt or bleed, but it does itch occasionally.

fallbackResponses: I just want to make sure it's nothing dangerous.
Does it look like cancer to you?
I can tell you about how it changed.
evaluationFocus: Identify risk factors for skin cancer

name: David Chen
complaint: Persistent sadness and lack of energy
personality: Quiet, avoids eye contact, speaks slowly
summary: Young professional presenting with classic signs of Major Depressive Disorder.
history: No prior psychiatric history, stressful job

keyFacts.label: Hidden clue
keyFacts.value: Has thought about 'not waking up' but denies active plan

expectedQuestions: How is your sleep and appetite?
Do you still enjoy your hobbies?
Have you had any thoughts of harming yourself?
recommendedTests: TSH and basic bloods to rule out organic causes
communicationGoals: Assess suicide risk gently but directly
hints: Be sure to ask directly about thoughts of self-harm or suicide.
Ask about sleep and appetite changes (SIGECAPS).

scriptedAnswers.answer: I fall asleep okay but wake up at 3 AM every day and can't go back to sleep.
I used to love playing guitar and gaming, but I haven't touched either in months. Nothing feels fun anymore.
I don't have a plan to kill myself, but sometimes I wish I just wouldn't wake up in the morning.

fallbackResponses: Everything just feels exhausting.
I don't know why I feel this way.
I can answer questions about my sleep or mood.

name: Martha Jenkins
complaint: Sudden severe pain in right eye and blurry vision
personality: In extreme distress, covering her eye
summary: Elderly patient with acute angle-closure glaucoma presenting with severe eye pain and halos.

keyFacts.label: Onset
keyFacts.value: Started 3 hours ago while in a dark cinema

keyFacts.label: Hidden clue
keyFacts.value: Right pupil is mid-dilated and fixed

expectedQuestions: What were you doing when it started?
Are you seeing halos around lights?
communicationGoals: Explain the need for urgent pressure-lowering treatment
hints: Ask about visual disturbances like halos around lights.
Ask what environment she was in when it started.

scriptedAnswers.answer: I was at the movie theater. It was dark, and suddenly my right eye started throbbing.
My vision is very blurry in that eye, and when I look at lights I see rainbow halos around them.
Yes, the pain is so bad it's making me feel very sick to my stomach.

fallbackResponses: My eye feels like it's going to pop.
Please do something for the pain.
I can't see properly out of my right eye.
evaluationFocus: Identify the trigger (pupillary dilation in the dark)

name: Robert Vance
complaint: Burning stomach pain and dark, tarry stools
summary: Patient with bleeding peptic ulcer presenting with melena and epigastric pain.
vitals: Temp 36.9 C, HR 105, RR 18, BP 105/65 (lying down), SpO2 98%
history: Frequent NSAID use for back pain, drinks 3-4 beers daily

keyFacts.label: Onset
keyFacts.value: Stomach pain for weeks, dark stools started yesterday

keyFacts.label: Hidden clue
keyFacts.value: Pain improves slightly right after eating but returns later

expectedQuestions: What color are your bowel movements?
Do you take ibuprofen or aspirin?
Does eating make the pain better or worse?
hints: Ask about the use of pain medications like NSAIDs.
Ask if the pain relates to meals.

scriptedAnswers.answer: Since yesterday, my bowel movements have been completely black and sticky, like tar.
I take about 800mg of ibuprofen a few times a day for my chronic back pain.
Strangely, the burning pain gets a bit better right after I eat, but then comes back a couple of hours later.
I felt really lightheaded and dizzy when I got out of bed this morning.

fallbackResponses: I feel very weak and tired today.
My stomach has been burning for weeks.
You can ask about my digestion or medications.

name: Carlos Gomez
complaint: Excruciating pain in left flank radiating to groin
personality: Restless, pacing the room, unable to sit still
summary: Classic presentation of nephrolithiasis (kidney stones) with renal colic.
history: No significant past medical history, poor hydration on job site

keyFacts.label: Hidden clue
keyFacts.value: Patient cannot find a comfortable position

expectedQuestions: Have you noticed blood in your urine?
Are you able to find a comfortable position?
communicationGoals: Explain the nature of renal colic
hints: Ask about changes in his urine color.
Note his behavior (pacing/restless) compared to peritonitis (still).

scriptedAnswers.answer: It starts in my left lower back and shoots straight down into my groin.
Yes, when I peed an hour ago it looked pinkish, like there was blood in it.
No! I can't sit still, I keep walking around but nothing makes the pain better.

fallbackResponses: Please give me something for the pain, it's a 10 out of 10.
I've never felt anything like this.
I just want the pain to stop.
evaluationFocus: Diagnose renal colic based on radiation and restlessness

name: Chloe Bennett
complaint: Severe headache and swollen hands/face at 34 weeks pregnant
summary: Primigravida patient presenting with symptoms of severe preeclampsia.

keyFacts.label: Onset
keyFacts.value: Swelling for 3 days, headache started today

keyFacts.label: Associated symptoms
keyFacts.value: Visual spots, right upper quadrant abdominal pain

keyFacts.label: Hidden clue
keyFacts.value: Pain under the right ribs (liver capsule stretching)

expectedQuestions: Are you having any changes in your vision?
Do you have any pain under your right ribs?
communicationGoals: Explain the serious nature of the condition without causing panic
hints: Ask about right upper quadrant or rib pain.
Ask about visual disturbances.

scriptedAnswers.answer: Yes, I keep seeing little flashing spots in my eyes today.
I have this dull ache right under my ribs on the right side. I thought it was just the baby kicking.
The baby is still moving, but maybe a little less than yesterday.
My feet have been swollen for a while, but today my face is puffy and I couldn't get my rings off.

fallbackResponses: Is my baby going to be okay?
My head is really pounding.
You can ask about my pregnancy or my symptoms.
evaluationFocus: Identify RUQ pain as a sign of liver involvement

name: Oliver Davies
summary: Classic case of acute otitis media following an upper respiratory tract infection.

keyFacts.label: Onset
keyFacts.value: Started pulling at his right ear last night

keyFacts.label: Hidden clue
keyFacts.value: Not eating well because swallowing hurts his ear

expectedQuestions: Has he had a cold recently?
Is there any discharge from the ear?
Is he eating and drinking normally?
hints: Ask about recent colds or runny nose.
Ask if there's any fluid coming out of the ear.

scriptedAnswers.answer: (Parent) Yes, he's had a runny nose and a mild cough for the last four days.
(Parent) No, I haven't seen any fluid or blood coming out of the ear.
(Parent) He is drinking milk, but he refuses to eat solid food. I think chewing hurts.

fallbackResponses: (Parent) He was up all night crying and holding his ear.
(Parent) Should I give him antibiotics?
(Child) My ear hurts!

name: Sophia Lin
complaint: Swelling of lips and difficulty breathing after eating
summary: Anaphylaxis triggered by a hidden food allergen, requiring immediate adrenaline.
vitals: Temp 36.9 C, HR 125, RR 28, BP 85/50, SpO2 90% on room air
history: Known peanut allergy, carries an EpiPen but forgot it today

keyFacts.label: Onset
keyFacts.value: 15 minutes ago after eating a cookie at a bakery

keyFacts.label: Associated symptoms
keyFacts.value: Hives on chest, lip swelling, wheezing, dizziness

keyFacts.label: Hidden clue
keyFacts.value: Hypotension (BP 85/50) indicates systemic shock, not just an allergic reaction

recommendedTests: Clinical diagnosis (no tests should delay treatment)
communicationGoals: Communicate urgency to the medical team
hints: Ask about known allergies and what she ate.
Check if she has self-administered any medication.

scriptedAnswers.answer: I ate... a chocolate cookie... from a bakery... 15 minutes ago.
I'm allergic... to peanuts. I think... cross-contamination.
I forgot... my EpiPen... at home. Please help.
I feel... really dizzy... like I'm going to pass out.

fallbackResponses: I can't... breathe...
My throat... is closing...
Help... me...
evaluationFocus: Identify the need for IM epinephrine over antihistamines

name: Cardio Patient 1
complaint: Chest pain and shortness of breath
hints: Ask if the pain moves to the jaw or arm.
Ask about sweating.

scriptedAnswers.answer: The pain seems to go down my left arm.
Yes, I feel very clammy and sweaty.
I feel like I can't catch my breath properly.

fallbackResponses: My chest feels very tight.
I'm worried it's a heart attack.
